Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Anchor tower to bottom so words build up from foundation

words stack upward — visually builds from the ground up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dynamic rise distance: each row rises from proportional depth below base

Bottom row rises 40px, top row rises (N-1)*50+40px — words physically
travel from the foundation level up to their final position.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Increase towerRowIn translateY for more dramatic rise from base

60px travel distance makes each row visibly rise up from below
instead of barely-noticeable 20px nudge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix word placement to build tower from base

Iterate placement loop bottom→top so base rows (wide, appear first in
animation) win ties over observation deck rows. Big/prominent words now
land at the foundation and visibly appear first as the tower builds up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make tower build animation visibly bottom-to-top

Increase row stagger from 40ms to 100ms and reduce per-row duration
from 500ms to 400ms, making the cascading bottom-to-top build effect
clearly perceivable (~1.1s total for 8 rows).

fix(dispatch): add --setting-sources to skip user-level plugins for Workers (#72)

Workers inherit user-level hooks (e.g. context-mode) which waste context
tokens and interfere with Read→Edit flow. Using --setting-sources project,local
ensures Workers only load project and local settings.

Part of #67

commit.message:
Fix channelId extraction + add AICD_DIAG debug API

- layer1.js: extractPageChannelId now also checks
  metadata.channelMetadataRenderer.externalId (reliable across YouTube
  versions) before falling back to URL regex; prior fix only checked the
  older c4TabbedHeaderRenderer path which newer channel pages don't have
- background.js: log channelId in STORE_FEEDBACK handler so service worker
  console immediately shows whether channelId is empty or populated
- content.js: add window.postMessage debug API — send
  {type:"AICD_DIAG"} or {type:"AICD_DIAG", videoId:"..."} from the
  DevTools console to dump pageChannelId, metaCache, channel_rep storage,
  and per-video L3 scores

- Ch 3: Methodology (8,713 words, DSR framework + 9 formal definitions)
- Ch 4: Results (5,031 words, 9 theorems in unified narrative)

Merge literature review halves into single file, remove temp parts.
Update plan: Phase III+IV complete, 9/10 quality gates PASS (58,010 words).

commit.message:
Add Niya archaeological clothing to Hanfu page; rewrite Lantern Festival origins

Hanfu page: New "Niya: Hanfu at the Edge of the Desert" section (EN/TC/SC)
documenting the 1995 excavation of tomb 95MNIM5 (c. 60-130 CE) at the Niya
site in Xinjiang — ruins of Jingjue, one of the 36 Western Region kingdoms.
Covers the silk robe reconstruction by Mayke Wagner (DAI), woollen trousers,
embroidered socks, and the "Five Stars Rise in the East" brocade. Three
archaeological photos included. New FAQ entry added.

Spring Festival page: Completely rewrote the Lantern Festival section (EN/TC/SC)
to correct the Buddhist origin myth. Now documents:
- Emperor Wu's Taiyi worship (104 BCE) predating Buddhism by 2+ centuries
- The forged Hanfa Ben Nei Zhuan as source of the Buddhist claim
- Etymology of 元宵 encoding lunar astronomical knowledge (小月為宵)
- Practical moonlight connection to night festivities
- Tang Dynasty curfew lifting (金吾不禁夜)
New FAQ entry on Yuanxiao's indigenous Chinese origins.

fix: audit — kill-safety, dead code, and DRY refactors in quality pipeline

Kill child test process on signal, block signals during atexit cleanup,
handle SIGHUP. Fix git_commit fallback scope, _run_analyzer tautology,
unreachable reviewer fallback. Extract _find_override() and gate_label()
to eliminate duplicated logic.

Fix addAddress failing on "Address already assigned" error

Newer kernels return "Address already assigned" instead of "File exists"
when an IP is already present on an interface. This caused configureNetwork
to bail before adding iptables forwarding rules, breaking traffic forwarding.

commit.message:
Add hexagonal.py: hexagon-tiling approach for zigzag graphene nanoflakes

Replaces the lattice-cut approach in hexagonal.py with a hexagon-tiling
method that guarantees zigzag edges by construction for any ring count.
Hexagon centres are placed using correct axial cube coordinates with
primitive vectors along the 30° and 90° edge-sharing neighbour directions,
so shared vertices are exact in floating-point and deduplication is reliable.
Updates all README and CLAUDE.md files to document the new script.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: pass plaintext API key to initial sync to avoid decrypt race

and getting undefined for apiKeyEncrypted during initial sync. Now the
POST route passes the plaintext key directly, bypassing decrypt.

test(simse-tui): migrate input tests to PTY harness

Migrate all 8 input.rs e2e tests from SimseTestHarness to real PTY tests
that spawn the actual binary. Tests cover typing, backspace, delete, arrow
key cursor movement, paste (bracketed paste mode), history up/down,
Ctrl+C quit behavior, and Alt+Backspace word deletion.

Key changes to the PTY harness:
- Replace ratatui-testlib ScreenState with vt100-ctt Parser for accurate
  terminal emulation (ScreenState was missing CSI J/K erase handlers,
  causing stale content after ratatui redraws)
- Add flush() after all write operations for reliable key delivery
- Add is_running() method for process exit detection
- Add Alt+Backspace encoding (ESC + DEL) to encode_key

commit.message:
TICKET-095: WebRTC P2P network layer (#96) (#96)

* TICKET-095: WebRTC P2P network layer for arena online play

Add DataChannelTransport to @pulse-ts/network that wraps a single
RTCDataChannel as a Transport for 1v1 P2P communication. Replace the
WebSocket relay-based online play with WebRTC peer connections:
the lobby phase connects to the Lambda signaling server, creates/joins
lobbies, performs SDP/ICE exchange, and establishes a DataChannel before
the game starts. ArenaNode now accepts a Transport directly instead of
a WebSocket URL, using useConnection() for transport-agnostic networking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* TICKET-095: Mark ticket as done

feat: Optimize GARCH web platform with improved reports and UX

This commit includes three major optimizations and the Basic GARCH Web platform integration:

## 1. Unified Performance Metrics Labels
- Change table headers from "未套保/套保后" to "传统套保 (h=1)/动态套保 (GARCH)"
- Update HTML report tables and metric cards (report_generator.py)
- Update CSV/Excel report column names (analyzer.py)
- Remove all ambiguous "unhedged/hedged" terminology

## 2. Improved ZIP File Naming
- Change from {model_type}_report_{timestamp}.zip to YYYYMMDD_{commodity}_{model}.zip
- Add extract_commodity_name() function to auto-detect commodity from filename
- Example: 20260305_MEG_Basic_GARCH.zip
- Support commodity detection: MEG, PP, PE, PVC, PTA (fallback to "通用")

## 3. Fixed Report Image Paths for Web View
- Modify /report endpoint to dynamically fix image paths using regex
- Add /report-images/<path:filename> route to serve images
- Convert relative paths (figures/xxx.png) to absolute web paths
- Images now display correctly when viewing reports through browser

## 4. Basic GARCH Web Platform Integration (Previous work)
- Refactor models from wrapper pattern to runner pattern
- Add lib/basic_garch_analyzer/ module with structured components
- Add lib/model_runners/ for unified model execution interface
- Add lib/shared_backtest/ for common backtest utilities
- Update models/ __init__.py to use new MODEL_RUNNERS interface
- Move old wrapper files to _backup_old_wrappers/
- Add matplotlib backend configuration to prevent threading issues
- Improve frontend JS with better error handling and status updates

## Testing
- Add test_optimizations.py for unit tests
- Add test_end_to_end.py for integration tests
- All tests passing ✓

## Documentation
- Add OPTIMIZATION_SUMMARY.md with detailed change documentation
- Add migration guides in lib/basic_garch_analyzer/MIGRATION.md
- Add changelog in lib/basic_garch_analyzer/CHANGELOG.md

commit.message:
Fix L3 on channel pages (ytd-grid-video-renderer + channelId extraction)

Three root causes why channel /videos pages didn't work:

1. ytd-grid-video-renderer not in selectors — channel pages use this element
   type instead of ytd-rich-item-renderer. Added to querySelectorAll and all
   CSS badge/position selectors.

2. gridVideoRenderer uses shortBylineText for channel info — buildMeta() only
   tried ownerText and longBylineText. Added shortBylineText as third fallback
   so channelId is extracted correctly from channel-page ytInitialData.

3. No page-level channelId fallback — on a channel page, cards often omit
   per-card channel info since it's implied. Added _pageChannelId extracted
   from ytInitialData.header.c4TabbedHeaderRenderer.channelId (and URL
   fallback for /channel/UCxxx paths). getChannelId() uses it as last resort.
   invalidateMetaCache() resets it on SPA navigation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Phase 4 ✅: T3+T4 passed; Fast-DDS + GZ_IP=127.0.0.1 fixes

T3 (Gazebo loads, /clock active) and T4 (cmd_vel→odom changes) now pass.

Root causes fixed:
- CycloneDDS rclpy.init() hangs on hosts with many bridge/veth interfaces
  (multicast join blocks). Switched to rmw_fastrtps_cpp in docker-compose.yml
  and both Dockerfiles. Updated D3 in development-plan.md.
- gz-transport also uses multicast for peer discovery. GZ_IP=127.0.0.1 forces
  it to loopback, enabling ros_gz_bridge to connect to Gazebo.
- Mounted ./scripts into containers as ~/ros2_ws/scripts so test_t4.py
  is accessible at the documented path.

commit.message:
Throttle V (voice input) timestamp updates to 1/sec

Audio buffer tap was dispatching to main thread on every buffer
(hundreds/sec), flooding the run loop and blocking Apple
Dictation from producing text. Now throttled to once per second.

- scraper: fetch_fullcontents() 追加

continuous=true so recognition keeps listening for multiple attempts.

commit.message:
Add description entry sheet for Add Description flow

When "Add Description" is tapped in the no-description prompt, a new
bottom sheet slides up showing the item price, a text input, and a
Confirm button. The pending price is stored temporarily; on confirm
the item is added to the cart with the entered description. The sheet
reuses the overlay/sheet pattern so keyboard avoidance works automatically.
